methodsAttempted.query: site:taltech.ee TalTech artificial intelligence AI policy students teaching learning generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official TalTech AI Focus Center pages and the TalTech good-practices academic-information page.
robots.txt allows public pages and disallows only /api/*.
robots.txt has no disallow rule for public AI Focus Center pages.

artifacts.summary: Official TalTech AI Focus Center and academic-information pages provide policy-specific AI guidance for teaching, learning, students, researchers, and university-managed AI tools. A news item and the general homepage were recorded as rejected because they were not suitable policy sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI at TalTech | AI Focus Center

artifacts.claimText: TalTech tells lecturers to explain acceptable AI-based software use in assessment, teaching, learning, and homework, and course-level permission or prohibition should be specified in the extended syllabus.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The lecturers shall explain to students the acceptable use of AI-based software in assessments, teaching, learning and/or homework. Information on this can be found in the extended course description.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: General principles for using artificial intelligence in teaching and learning
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TalTech permits AI applications as support tools for inspiration, refinement, translation, early-stage learning support, and editing, but says they must not be used for extensive thesis sections, fabricated data, or substantive arguments.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI applications can be used as a source of inspiration, a tool for evaluating and refining ideas, for translation, and to support learning during the early stages of the work. AI applications can also be helpful in editing student-generated text during the final stages of work. However, AI applications must not be used for the preparation of extensive sections of a graduation thesis (e.g. an entire chapter), fabricating data for analysis, or generating substantive arguments.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: General principles for using artificial intelligence in teaching and learning
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TalTech says substantive AI output must be cited or described as a method, and learners remain responsible for accuracy, quality, analysis results, and references.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If the output from the application is used substantively (such as a section suggested by an AI tool or an image generated by an image creator), the application used must be properly cited as a method. A learner is fully responsible for the accuracy and quality of all information, research material and the results of analysis submitted for assessment, as well as the correctness of all references.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: General principles for using artificial intelligence in teaching and learning
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TalTech permits AI technologies in teaching and learning only when they comply with personal data processing, data privacy, and cybersecurity regulations and their use does not violate regulations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The university permits the use of AI technologies in teaching and learning, provided they comply with personal data processing, data privacy, and cybersecurity regulations, and their use by lecturers or students does not violate the regulations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: General principles for using artificial intelligence in teaching and learning
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TalTech states that students can use the web version of Microsoft Copilot with a UNI-ID account and says Copilot data entered through that account is protected in a secure isolated environment.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At the university, students can use the web version of Microsoft Copilot with their UNI-ID account. When using Microsoft Copilot with a UNI-ID account, all entered data is protected and kept in a secure and isolated environment.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TalTech's ChatGPT Edu usage rules say users should use ChatGPT primarily for ideas, drafts, and helper text, must not delegate critical decisions to the model, and must not upload sensitive personal, confidential, security-critical, or unauthorized copyrighted content.

citation.citationTitle: AI at TalTech | AI Focus Center
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI at TalTech | AI Focus Center
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use ChatGPT primarily for ideas, drafts and helper text, not for making final decisions. Do not delegate critical decision-making to the model – final decisions must always be made by a human. Do not use the tool in a way that could endanger the university’s reputation, security or legal compliance.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: ChatGPT EDU Usage Rules - General principles

artifacts.claimText: TalTech researcher guidance says AI-created texts and analyses should be clearly indicated and documented, and AI must not replace the researcher's critical thinking or act as a hidden author.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Reproducibility and documentation – texts and analyses created with the help of AI should be clearly indicated and documented. Ethics and reliability – AI must not replace the researcher’s critical thinking or act as a hidden author.

Approved as a narrow claim about supported and prohibited AI uses in teaching and learning; no broader misconduct inference added.
Approved because the claim closely follows the official wording on substantive AI output and learner responsibility.
Approved as a privacy/compliance condition for AI use in teaching and learning, scoped to the source text.
Approved as a student-tool availability and data-protection claim based on the official student AI Focus Center page.

artifacts.title: Tallinn University of Technology (TalTech) AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Official TalTech AI Focus Center and academic-information sources support staged claims on course-level AI-use disclosure, academic integrity limits, citation and responsibility for substantive AI output, privacy and cybersecurity compliance, student Microsoft Copilot access, ChatGPT Edu use rules, and researcher transparency/documentation expectations.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The bundle stages agent-reviewed candidates only; it does not publish canonical claims.
Official news and generic homepage sources were not used as policy evidence.
